markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games’ decision to delay Grand Theft Auto VI until November 19, 2026, after two prior postponements, has extended the window for unrelated events to resolve ahead of launch and sharpened trader focus on the studio’s marketing cadence. With the next major Take-Two earnings call arriving in late May, any confirmation of steady progress or hints at a summer trailer drop could reinforce the current November target and keep the market window open. Historical patterns show Rockstar typically unveils additional trailers six to nine months before release, making an August reveal a key near-term catalyst that would further influence which pop-culture or geopolitical milestones traders expect to occur first.

The resolution source for Bitcoin is Binance, specifically the BTCUSDT "High" prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent mistrial in Harvey Weinstein’s third New York rape retrial has anchored trader consensus around no additional prison time, currently implied at 78.8 percent. The deadlocked jury on May 15, 2026, marked the latest in a series of inconclusive outcomes after his original 2020 conviction was overturned on appeal, leaving prosecutors to decide whether to pursue a fourth trial at a June 24 hearing. Weinstein, now 74, continues serving a separate 16-year California sentence that remains under appeal, while time already served and documented health concerns narrow the window for meaningful further incarceration. Market sentiment reflects these procedural hurdles and the high bar for securing additional convictions in a case that has spanned multiple jurisdictions.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Colorado Avalanche and Carolina Hurricanes remain neck-and-neck as the clear frontrunners heading into the NHL conference finals, each benefiting from elite regular-season performances and deep playoff runs that have kept their implied probabilities tightly aligned. Colorado advanced with a sweep of the Los Angeles Kings and a decisive series win over the Minnesota Wild, leaning on stars like Nathan MacKinnon and Cale Makar plus proven Cup experience. Carolina has similarly dominated, dispatching the Ottawa Senators and Philadelphia Flyers while showcasing one of the league’s most balanced rosters. The Vegas Golden Knights and Montreal Canadiens trail as underdogs in their respective series against these powers, with recent momentum and defensive structure providing realistic upset paths but limiting their current market positioning.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?

eventMetadata.context_description: The tight clustering among France, Spain, and England as the leading contenders stems from their exceptional squad depth and recent international consistency heading into the expanded 48-team tournament. France maintains a slight edge through its blend of proven tournament winners and attacking talent led by Kylian Mbappé, while Spain benefits from reigning European Championship pedigree and a young core featuring Lamine Yamal and Pedri. England’s strong recent form and midfield control keep it competitive, with all three nations showing minimal separation in power rankings due to comparable roster health and group-stage matchups. Broader depth across Portugal, Brazil, and Argentina further compresses the field, as historical success and current form limit any single side from pulling away decisively.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping's firm grip on the Chinese Communist Party, secured through years of institutional reforms and leadership appointments, underpins the strong trader consensus against his removal before 2027. By abolishing presidential term limits in 2018 and maintaining unchallenged authority over the Politburo Standing Committee, Central Military Commission, and security apparatus, he has minimized factional opposition and established a precedent for extended rule. Recent analyses of elite dynamics ahead of the 21st Party Congress highlight ongoing consolidation rather than transition signals, with no verified developments such as health crises, policy reversals, or internal purges altering his position. While unpredictable events like sudden illness or elite maneuvering could still shift outcomes, the current pricing aligns with the absence of concrete indicators for departure within the timeframe.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.
